communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #P001706-052721
communications.communication:
Dear Sir or Madam:

In consideration of my appeal, sent July 14, 2021, of your processing of my May 27, 2021, request for your staff roster, and of future requests for records that contain names, identification numbers, or department/division/unit of public employees, please see Municipal Research and Services Center's February 24, 2020, guidance, "New Ruling Provides Guidance on `Specific Intelligence'": <https://mrsc.org/Home/Stay-Informed/MRSC-Insight/February-2020/New-Ruling-on-Specific-Intelligence.aspx>.  In it, author Steve Goss references West v. City of Tacoma, No. 51487-7-II (Published January 28, 2020), Division II of the Court of Appeals.

Reading that opinion, I find the following (pp25-26) to be of particular relevance to your processing of my request:

==== BEGIN QUOTED MATERIAL ================
Under RCW 42.56.240(1), specific intelligence information and specific investigative records may be exempt from production. This exemption is intended to protect the integrity of law enforcement investigations. Koenig v. Thurston County, 175 Wn.2d 837, 843, 287 P.3d 523 (2012). To qualify for this exemption, the record must be (1) specific information that is intelligence or investigative in nature; (2) compiled by an investigative, law enforcement, or penological agency; and (3) essential to law enforcement or the protection of privacy. RCW 42.56.240(1); Wade’s Eastside Gun Shop, Inc. v. Dep’t of Labor and Indus., 185 Wn.2d 270, 281, 372 P.3d 97 (2016).

Specific investigative records are records compiled from a specific investigation that focused on a particular party. Koenig, 175 Wn.2d at 843. A specific investigation is “designed to ferret out criminal activity or to shed light on some other allegation of malfeasance.” Columbian Publ’g Co. v. City of Vancouver, 36 Wn. App. 25, 31, 671 P.2d 280 (1983). The City does not argue that the redacted documents were investigative records, and because the redacted records did not result from a particular investigation, these records cannot be specific investigative records. Thus, our first focus is on the question of whether the information is “specific intelligence information.”

“Specific intelligence information” is not defined in the PRA. “[T]he term ‘specific’ in the exemption for specific intelligence information must be read to require not that the information concern particular individuals, but that it disclose particular methods or procedures for gathering or evaluating intelligence information.” Haines-Marchel v. Dep’t of Corr., 183 Wn. App. 655, 669, 334 P.3d 99 (2014).

In King County v. Sheehan, we held that a list of police officers’ names was not specific intelligence information. 114 Wn. App. 325, 337-38, 57 P.3d 307 (2002). Because the PRA does not define the term “specific intelligence information,” we looked to a dictionary definition to determine its ordinary meaning. King County, 114 Wn. App. at 337. We defined intelligence in this context as “‘the gathering or distribution of information, especially secret information,’ or ‘information about an enemy’ or ‘the evaluated conclusions drawn from such information.’” King County, 114 Wn. App. at 337 (quoting RANDOM HOUSE UNABRIDGED DICTIONARY 990 (2d ed. 1993)). Further, we held that the exemption applied to specific intelligence information, which suggested an even narrower interpretation of the exemption. King County, 114 Wn. App.  at 337.
==== END QUOTED MATERIAL ================

The material you redacted is not records compiled from a specific investigation that focused on a particular party.  The "specific intelligence information" exemption described in RCW 42.56.240(1) does not apply.

Cordially,
Phil Mocek

communications.subject: Appeal of Public Records Request No. P001706-052721
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Mocek,

Your appeal of the City of Bellevue's response to public records request number P001706-052721 was forwarded to me yesterday, July 22, 2021. I have carefully considered the appeal and determined that the redactions made were appropriate. As clarified in the attached document, no employee names or badge numbers were redacted. Lines without names and/or numbers reflect that those positions are currently vacant. The names of units with undercover officers were redacted under RCW 42.56.240(1) because the nondisclosure of this information is essential to protect the integrity of law enforcement activities and to maintain officer safety as it may reveal the identity of individuals who are or were performing an undercover or covert law enforcement activity.

The original format for the staff roster you requested is Microsoft Word. This information does not currently exist in another format but, as a courtesy, the City can create an Excel document with the information. If that is agreeable, we will send you this information again in Excel format.

Thank you,

Kathleen

Kathleen Kline
Assistant City Attorney
City of Bellevue
450 110th Avenue NE
P.O. Box 90012
Bellevue, WA 98009
Phone: 425-452-4085
kkline@bellevuewa.gov<mailto:kkline@bellevuewa.gov>

If you are not an intended recipient of this message or if this message has been addressed to you in error, please immediately notify the sender and delete this message and any attachments. Use, dissemination, distribution, or reproduction of this message by unintended recipients is not authorized and may be unlawful.

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #P001706-052721
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Kline:

I am surprised to learn that your agency's staff roster is maintained as a word processor file rather than in a database of some sort.  If that is truly the case, then you are under no obligation to transform it for me.  If, however, it is in a database and the word processor file is generated from data in that database, then I ask that you export to a spreadsheet file instead of to a word processor file.

Names of units, with or without undercover officers, are not records compiled from a specific investigation that focused on a particular party.  42.56.240(1), the "specific intelligence information" exemption, does not apply, regardless of whether or not nondisclosure of that information is essential to protect the integrity of law enforcement activities or to maintain officer safety.

Please see Municipal Research and Services Center's February 24, 2020, guidance, "New Ruling Provides Guidance on `Specific Intelligence'": <https://mrsc.org/Home/Stay-Informed/MRSC-Insight/February-2020/New-Ruling-on-Specific-Intelligence.aspx>. In it, author Steve Goss references West v. City of Tacoma, No. 51487-7-II (Published January 28, 2020), Division II of the Court of Appeals.

Reading that opinion, I find the following (pp25-26) to be of particular relevance to your processing of my request:

==== BEGIN QUOTED MATERIAL ================
Under RCW 42.56.240(1), specific intelligence information and specific investigative records may be exempt from production. This exemption is intended to protect the integrity of law enforcement investigations. Koenig v. Thurston County, 175 Wn.2d 837, 843, 287 P.3d 523 (2012). To qualify for this exemption, the record must be (1) specific information that is intelligence or investigative in nature; (2) compiled by an investigative, law enforcement, or penological agency; and (3) essential to law enforcement or the protection of privacy. RCW 42.56.240(1); Wade’s Eastside Gun Shop, Inc. v. Dep’t of Labor and Indus., 185 Wn.2d 270, 281, 372 P.3d 97 (2016).

Specific investigative records are records compiled from a specific investigation that focused on a particular party. Koenig, 175 Wn.2d at 843. A specific investigation is “designed to ferret out criminal activity or to shed light on some other allegation of malfeasance.” Columbian Publ’g Co. v. City of Vancouver, 36 Wn. App. 25, 31, 671 P.2d 280 (1983). The City does not argue that the redacted documents were investigative records, and because the redacted records did not result from a particular investigation, these records cannot be specific investigative records. Thus, our first focus is on the question of whether the information is “specific intelligence information.”

“Specific intelligence information” is not defined in the PRA. “[T]he term ‘specific’ in the exemption for specific intelligence information must be read to require not that the information concern particular individuals, but that it disclose particular methods or procedures for gathering or evaluating intelligence information.” Haines-Marchel v. Dep’t of Corr., 183 Wn. App. 655, 669, 334 P.3d 99 (2014).

In King County v. Sheehan, we held that a list of police officers’ names was not specific intelligence information. 114 Wn. App. 325, 337-38, 57 P.3d 307 (2002). Because the PRA does not define the term “specific intelligence information,” we looked to a dictionary definition to determine its ordinary meaning. King County, 114 Wn. App. at 337. We defined intelligence in this context as “‘the gathering or distribution of information, especially secret information,’ or ‘information about an enemy’ or ‘the evaluated conclusions drawn from such information.’” King County, 114 Wn. App. at 337 (quoting RANDOM HOUSE UNABRIDGED DICTIONARY 990 (2d ed. 1993)). Further, we held that the exemption applied to specific intelligence information, which suggested an even narrower interpretation of the exemption. King County, 114 Wn. App. at 337.
==== END QUOTED MATERIAL ================

Cordially,
Phil Mocek

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #P001706-052721
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Mocek,

Thank you for your email. Again, the existing police department staff roster (employee list), that would show all the information you requested (first and last names, job titles, areas of assignment, and badge, serial, and other identifying numbers) is only maintained as a word document and was not generated from a database. As a courtesy, we have compiled the datasets you requested by creating a new record from multiple sources. The attached excel and csv documents have the (1) first name, (2) last name, (3) title, (4) badge number, and (5) area of assignment for police department staff.

RCW 42.56.240(1) exempts both “intelligence information” and “investigative records” compiled by a law enforcement agency if the information is “essential to effective law enforcement” or needed to protect a person’s privacy rights.” Intelligence information includes records that may reveal particular methods or procedures for gathering or evaluating intelligence information, the disclosure of which would harm future law enforcement efforts. See Haines-Marchel v. State, Dept. of Corrections, 183 Wn. App. 655, 334 P.3d 99 (2014) (concluding identity of informants and criteria used to evaluate the reliability of informants was specific intelligence information); Gronquist v. State, Department of Corrections, 177 Wn. App. 389, 313 P.3d 416 (2013), review denied, 180 Wn.2d 1004, 321 P.3d 1207 (2014) (concluding prison surveillance recordings was intelligence information because they would reveal information about investigative methods). As the quote you excerpt in your email from the recent West case makes clear, “[T]he term ‘specific’ in the exemption for specific intelligence information must be read to require not that the information concern particular individuals, but that it disclose particular methods or procedures for gathering or evaluating intelligence information.” West v. City of Tacoma, 12 Wn. App. 2d 45, 71, 334 P.3d 99 (2020), quoting Haines-Marchel, 183 Wn. App. at 669.

Kathleen

Kathleen Kline
Assistant City Attorney
City of Bellevue
450 110th Avenue NE
P.O. Box 90012
Bellevue, WA 98009
Phone: 425-452-4085
kkline@bellevuewa.gov<mailto:kkline@bellevuewa.gov>

communications.subject: Police Records Request :: P001629-031226
communications.communication:
Dear Phil Mocek, 
 
Thank you for submitting your request for public records to the City of Bellevue Police Department. Your request was received in our office on 3/12/2026 2:27:17 PM and given the reference number P001629-031226 for tracking purposes. The details of your request are below.  
 
Bellevue Police Department 
Washington Public Records Act Office 
P.O. Box 90012 
Bellevue, WA 98004 

March 12, 2026 

To Whom It May Concern: 
## PRESERVATION DEMAND ## 
Pursuant to O'Neill v. City of Shoreline, 170 Wn.2d 138 (2010), and WAC 44-14-03005, your agency's obligation to preserve all records responsive to this request attaches upon receipt. 
Please immediately: 

1. Suspend any automated deletion, overwrite, or purge process that would destroy records falling within the scope of this request, whether operating on your agency's own infrastructure or on vendor-managed infrastructure over which your agency holds a contractual right of access or retrieval. 

2. Issue a written instruction to your ALPR vendor(s) directing them to halt any automated deletion of records within the scope of this request pending its resolution. 
## VENDOR-HELD RECORDS ##. 

In Rodriguez v. City of Stanwood & Sedro-Woolley (Skagit County Superior Court, No. 22-2-00477-29, Nov. 6, 2025), the court held that ALPR data held by a third-party SaaS vendor on behalf of a government agency is a public record subject to PRA disclosure, and that vendor custody does not relieve the agency of its production obligation. That ruling is currently on appeal to the Washington Court of Appeals, Division 1; the appeal does not stay the disclosure obligation. Each request below that encompasses vendor-held records is made on this basis. Please retrieve and produce all responsive records held by your ALPR vendor(s) on your agency's behalf. 

## ELECTRONIC REPRODUCTION AND THE DUTY TO PRODUCE ## 

I seek existing records only. I understand that no agency is required to create a new record in response to a PRA request. However, producing a screenshot or other faithful reproduction of an existing electronic display is not creating a new record — it is reproducing an existing one, in the same manner as photocopying or scanning a paper document. Where a requested record exists only as a display within a software interface, a screenshot or faithful reproduction of that display, or an export of that display to any available file format (including CSV, PDF, or JSON), is an acceptable and required form of production. 

## FEE REQUEST ## 

I am requesting these records for research and public interest purposes, submitted through MuckRock, a nonprofit journalism platform. I request that your agency consider waiving any fees given that this request is submitted for research and public interest purposes and that electronic production of the requested records should entail minimal or no actual cost under RCW 42.56.120. For any anticipated costs, please notify me before incurring them so that I may prioritize or narrow the request. This notification requirement should not be construed as authorization to delay production of records for which no fee will be incurred pending resolution of fees for other records in this request 

## RECORDS REQUESTED ## 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 
1. All existing ALPR scan records (e.g., stored in your agency's ALPR system or any system used to store, process, or query ALPR data, such as Axon Evidence.com, Flock Safety portal, or Motorola/Vigilant), including all metadata fields associated with each record as captured and stored by the system. This includes but is not limited to: license plate number, timestamp, GPS coordinates, camera or device identifier, lane identifier, direction of travel, confidence score, vehicle make, vehicle model, vehicle color, image file metadata, and any field capturing information about vehicle occupants or associated individuals. 
2. All system audit logs associated with the records in item 1, as natively maintained in your agency's systems or your vendor's systems, reflecting every query performed against your agency's ALPR system, including for each query: the date and time, the user identifier or badge number of the querying user, the Originating Agency Identifier (ORI) of the querying agency, and the entry in the "reason for search," "justification," "purpose," or equivalent field. Where no justification was entered for a query, the log entry reflecting that absence is itself a responsive record and should be produced. 

To facilitate partial rolling production while the full dataset is being compiled, please prioritize production of records associated with the following specific license plates. Prioritizing these plates does not limit or waive any part of this request: 

Seattle Police Department specialized and long-service fleet vehicles 
(from City of Seattle fleet records; plates may have been re-registered 
but vehicles are believed to remain in service): 
c. 50816D — Surveillance trailer (Skywatch Sentinel, 2010) 
d. 50887D — Hybrid command center (2007, est. replacement 2031) 
e. 54180D — Police armored vehicle (2011, est. replacement 2031) 
f. 43500D — Bomb squad trailer (2005) 
g. 26073D — Bomb containment vessel trailer (1997) 
h. 41461D — Communications van (2005) 
i. 44372D — Command vehicle (2005) 
j. 54148D — Riot fencing trailer (2011) 
k. 54362D — CSI van with generator (2012) 
l. 50295D — SWAT cutaway van (2008) 
m. 54117D — CSI van (2011) 
n. 24208D — Weapons vehicle (1987) 

Washington State fleet vehicles (by plate pattern): 
o. All plates with the prefix or suffix "WSP" 
p. All plates with the prefix "XMT" 

Other: 
a. BCH-478 
b. 826-ZVA 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 
Sincerely, 

Phil Mocek 

View request history, upload responsive documents, and report problems here: 
https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2238469/ 
Filed via MuckRock.com 
E-mail (Preferred): 206500-00859330@requests.muckrock.com 
PLEASE NOTE OUR NEW ADDRESS 
For mailed responses, please address (see note): 
MuckRock News 
DEPT MR 206500 
263 Huntington Ave 
Boston, MA 02115 
PLEASE NOTE: This request is not filed by a MuckRock staff member, but is being sent through MuckRock by the above in order to better track, share, and manage public records requests. Also note that improperly addressed (i.e., with the requester's name rather than "MuckRock News" and the department number) requests might be returned as undeliverable. 
The City must provide a preliminary response to your request within five (5) business days (RCW 42.56.520); the day the request is received does not count as one of the five (5) days. Weekends and holidays observed by the City are also excluded in the calculation. communications.subject: [Records Center] Police Records Request :: P001796-031926
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line. Anything below will not be viewable to staff. ---

"03/19/2026
P001796-031926
 
Phil Mocek,
 
 The Bellevue Police Department has received your public records request for the following:
N/A / Staff roster including but not limited to first and last names, job titles, areas of assignment, and badge, serial, and other identifying numbers. In order of preference, I would prefer that this data be provided as: OpenDocument spreadsheet, plain text with comma-separated-values (i.e., a "CSV" file), Excel spreadsheet, or the native electronic format of the software from which this information is retrieved in fulfillment of my request. I specifically request that you not create a new record for me by rendering to PDF or by printing your electronic record then scanning it to make a series of images of the printed data. (Unrecognized email flagged by CCO)
 
Please be advised, your request was sent to an email address that cannot accept responses and was flagged by the City Clerk's Office. In future, please create requests from your account or send an email to BPDRecords@bellevuewa.gov. 
 
In your request, you mentioned three other requests. Please be advised, we do not have a request with the reference number P001706-052721. Considering it would have been created in 2021 it is most likely past retention and no longer accessible because of its age. P001629-031226 was closed as a duplicate request to P001641-031226, which is an open request for different records. Please also be advised that we are not permitted to access websites or cloud storage to provide records, but we can provide them via our portal and you will be able to download them and place them into whichever system you prefer.
 
Your request is still considered open. You should anticipate an additional response by the end of business on 3/27/2026, if not sooner. Please be advised, our staff roster exists and is not a spreadsheet. 
 
If you have any questions regarding your disclosure request or any other matter, please contact the Bellevue Police Department Records Unit via the Public Records Portal or by telephone at (425) 452-6917. 
 
Thank you,
BPD Disclosure
Bellevue Police Department"

communications.communication:

03/24/2026 P001796-031926 Phil Mocek, 
Records responsive to your request are now available via the City of Bellevue Public Records Portal website. Pop-ups may need to be enabled on your internet browser to access your files for download. This concludes our search of records in the custody of the Bellevue Police Department. This is our final, definitive response and your request is closed.  As the city does not intend to further address the request, the Public Records Act one-year statute of limitations to seek judicial review has now started to run. Pursuant to the Public Records Act RCW 42.56, responsive records may be redacted or exempt. Please review the accompanying privilege log for further information, if such redactions or exemptions have been applied. Per the State Public Records Act (RCW 42.56.120) and City Public Records Act Rules (Section 6), a public records request may be considered abandoned and subsequently closed if a requestor fails to fulfill their obligation to claim an installment of records; claim the entire production of records; inspect the records; or fails to pay the deposit or final payment for requested records within 30 days of this notification. Please be advised, you may file a written appeal if you believe information has been incorrectly redacted or records have been incorrectly withheld. The appeal must include your name and address, a copy of the redacted document and a copy of this letter together with a brief statement identifying the basis of the appeal. Please mail your appeal to: Bellevue Police Department 
Attn: Records Supervisor 
P.O. Box 90012 
Bellevue, WA  98009-9012 If you require any further assistance from the Bellevue Police Department regarding this matter or have questions, please contact the Records Unit via the Public Records Portal or by telephone at (425) 452-6917 within 30 days of this notification. Thank you, Jessica Havens 
Bellevue Police Department 
RPC 4.4 Inadvertent Disclosure Notification: CR 26 (b) (6) Claw Back Provision *** Please read this notice carefully *** Dear legal practitioner: Please take notice that we take reasonable precautions to prevent the disclosure of privileged or confidential materials, including those protected by the attorney-client privilege and/or work product doctrine, when responding to request for records.  These precautions include, but are not limited to, carefully locating and assembling responsive records, performing an intra-departmental review of the records for purposes of determining whether they contain any privileged or confidential materials, forwarding the records to the County Prosecuting Attorney's Office for independent legal review, meeting and conferring with the Prosecuting Attorney's Office to compare and reconcile any conflicting results of our separate independent reviews and, if necessary, creating a withholding log.  Despite our having taken these precautions, occasionally a mistake is made and privileged or confidential documents are inadvertently produced.  To that end, we wanted to notify you that there is a possibility that the records being produced may contain privileged or confidential materials.  The inadvertent or unintentional production of privileged or confidential documents, without a privileged or confidentiality designation should not be considered a waiver in whole or in part of any claim of privileged or confidential treatment, and any document that initially is produced without bearing a privileged or confidentiality designation may later be so designated.  In the event that you believe you have received privileged or confidential materials, we would ask that you promptly notify us of this fact and take action as necessary to comply with the obligations set forth in Rule of Professional Conduct 4.4 and/or Civil Rule 26 (b)(6). 

 
To monitor the progress or update this request please log into the Bellevue Public Records Center (https://u8387778.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.VEsWrlMiJDg0mXafq1mEXbUWSWnJYM-2FRewwjUGmaQvl-2Foebzj8OR-2F9FwJY48-2FiaJ4jQOGvZL4X-2F2Gx-2Bi0NaDrzCGoxj77nEMQGv-2BZSBXn8Y-3Dw6aO_wlzVhu-2F-2FS2zzloS3j1z9ymqKcFaOfBK4aU1EFb6CkHmdlr9NP6HbpRIq1cW38tpigsMChXYrrMauWbIJ09Lvioh6bdq-2Bm-2FtLotP-2BZVCh0UUHcfBZT-2BkkwTbXE8VOhGsRAjfFechS2rTI6Ks1ng2ktT94vLAzmZqvzxmYl7JKNFVYbvy5Ui2PhmDbTSR7XQpu0llVxPtq3pGCuCkk22VL1wDFTkBQntM7jAioEVsQSCnEwfrwVB1f4-2FfdqO55-2FbmqxSaugGs1XG4P88MYYj8Mw709BZd9d7l8HBr-2BNRw2J-2Ff56J9L7-2FVG9ec6jm1vjiVFNfehc8e1Zen6gstm5SjMsUtoAap99gupzd-2B9T9d-2BE0yP0AVamriIgOB8-2FzmC-2F9Xtext55Yye3axIwIJLpe2qJA-3D-3D)

Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center.

March 24, 2026 Miriam Bhimani Miriam.bhimani@gmail.com  RE: Chicago Public Schools FOIA Request N019200-030826 Dear Miriam Bhimani: This letter is in reply to your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) Request seeking the following information: Supply the Investigative Memoranda with the following File Numbers: 
INV-09577 
INV-09620 
INV-09648 
INV-09654 
INV-09658 
INV-09678 
INV-09709 
INV-09712

INV-09742 
INV-09747 
INV-09746 
INV-09752 
INV-09751 
INV-09754 
INV-09755 
INV-09758 
INV-09760 
RESPONSE: Documents responsive to your FOIA request are available to download via the Chicago Public Schools FOIA Center. Please visit the following link and enter your username and password to access the records: http://cps.edu/About_CPS/Departments/Law/Pages/FOIARequest.aspx . Records have been redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7 (1)(b), “Private information, unless disclosure is required by another provision of this Act, a State or federal law or a court order,” and pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(c), “Personal information contained within public records, the disclosure of which would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy, unless the disclosure is consented to in writing by the individual subjects of the information. “Unwarranted invasion of personal privacy” means the disclosure of information that is highly personal or objectionable to a reasonable person and in which the subject’s right to privacy outweighs the legitimate public interest in obtaining the information. The disclosure of information that bears on the public duties of public employees and officials shall not be considered an invasion of personal privacy. 
Redactions were additionally made to conceal individual identities pursuant to Section 7(1)(d)(iv) which allows a public to withhold records/information that would unavoidably reveal the identity of a complainant or witness. nformation has additionally been redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7(a): Information specifically prohibited by federal or State law or rules and regulations implementing federal or state law. Specifically, the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA). Pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7.5(r) in accordance with Illinois Student Records Act (ISSRA). ISSRA prohibits the disclosure of “school student records” and defines a “school student record” as “any writing or other recorded information concerning a student and by which a student may be individually identified, maintained by a school or at its direction or by an employee of a school, regardless of how or where the information is stored. 105 ILCS 10/(d): Release of a minor’s record may only be authorized by the parent or guardian in accordance with the Illinois Student Records Act (105 ILCS 10/1 et seq.). Pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(a), this section exempts from disclosure information specifically prohibited from disclosure by federal or State law or rules and regulations implementing federal or State law. Specifically, the Abused and Neglected Child Reporting Act, section 325 ILCS 5/1, states that all records concerning reports of child abuse and neglect or records concerning referrals under this Act and all records generated as a result of such reports or referrals, shall be confidential and shall not be disclosed except as specifically authorized by this Act or other applicable law. Records have been redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7.5(v): vulnerability assessments, security measures, and response policies or plans that are designed to identify, prevent, or respond to potential attacks upon a community’s population or systems, facilities or installations, but only to the extent that disclosure could reasonably be expected to expose the vulnerability or jeopardize the effectiveness of the measures, policies, or plans, or the safety of the personnel who implement them or the public. Information exempt under this item may include such things as details pertaining to the mobilization or deployment of personnel or equipment, to the operation of communication systems or protocols, to cybersecurity vulnerabilities, or to tactical operations. Section 7.5(h) of the FOIA, exempts from disclosure “information the disclosure of which is exempted under the State Officials and Employees Ethics Act, and records of any lawfully created State or local inspector general’s office that would be exempt if created or obtained by an Executive Inspector General’s office.” 5 ILCS 140/7.5(h). Lastly, Section 7.5(v) of the Illinois Freedom of Information Act exempts from public disclosure the names and information of individuals who have applied for or received Firearm Owner’s Identification (FOID) cards under the Firearm Owners Identification Card Act. 
INV-09711 INV-09748 RESPONSE: The records you seek have been withheld as they are exempt from disclosure pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(m) which exempts communications between a public body and an attorney representing the public body that would not be subject to discovery in litigation, and materials prepared or compiled by or for a public body in anticipation of a criminal, civil or administrative proceeding upon the request of an attorney advising the public body. INV-09697 INV-09698 INV-09761 INV-09765 RESPONSE: The CPS FOIA Office conducted a due diligence search and there are no responsive records as no memorandums were created. In case of denial, pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a) you may file a Request for Review with the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at: Public Access Counselor; Office of the Attorney General; 500 South 2nd Street; Springfield, IL 62706 . You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court; 5 ILCS 140/11.  Thank you for your request. Sincerely, Ana Diaz Freedom of Information Act Officer Chicago Public Schools

title: Performance Evaluation (Monadnock Regional School District)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Performance Evaluation (Monadnock Regional School District)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of the most recent performance evaluation for the current district superintendent at the time of this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

communications.subject: Re: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Performance Evaluation (Monadnock Regional School District)
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

Please see attached.

Please note that any responsive records containing student educational
record information are exempt from disclosure under RSA 91-A:5, IV, which
protects confidential files and individual privacy. Furthermore, such
records are protected by the federal Family Educational Rights and Privacy
Act (FERPA), which prohibits the unauthorized release of personally
identifiable information from a student's education record.

Thank you,
Jeremy



-- 


Jeremy Rathbun

Superintendent of Schools

SAU93 - Monadnock Regional School District

This message contains confidential information and is intended only for the
individual named. If you are not the named addressee you should not
disseminate, distribute or copy this e-mail. Please notify the sender
immediately by e-mail if you have received this e-mail by mistake and
delete this e-mail from your system. If you are not the intended recipient,
you are notified that disclosing, copying, distributing or taking any
action in reliance on the contents of this information is strictly
prohibited.  The Right-To-Know Law provides that most e-mail communications
to or from School District employees regarding the business of the School
District are government records available to the public upon request.
Therefore, this e-mail communication may be subject to public disclosure.

title: WA ALPR: A: External Access Audit Logs (1/4) (Auburn Police Department)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: WA ALPR: A: External Access Audit Logs (1/4) (Auburn Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All system audit logs, as natively maintained in your agency's systems or your vendor's systems, reflecting access to your agency's ALPR system or any system used to store, process, or query ALPR data (e.g., Axon Evidence.com, Flock Safety portal, Motorola/Vigilant), by users affiliated with any agency other than your agency.

2. All system audit logs, as natively maintained, reflecting the Originating Agency Identifier (ORI) or agency name associated with each external query of your agency's ALPR system.

3. All system audit logs, as natively maintained, reflecting the user identifier, badge number, or account name associated with each query of your agency's ALPR system, whether the querying user was internal or external to your agency.

4. All records identifying which external agencies are currently authorized to access, query, or receive data from your agency's ALPR system, including any configuration record, permissions list, access control list, or authorized-agency registry maintained in your ALPR platform.  If this information exists only as a display within a software interface, a screenshot, faithful reproduction, or export of that display to any available file format is an acceptable and required form of production.

Note on vendor-held records: In Rodriguez v. City of Stanwood & Sedro-Woolley (Skagit County Superior Court, Nov. 6, 2025), the court held that ALPR data held by a third-party SaaS vendor on behalf of a government agency is a public record subject to PRA disclosure. Vendor custody does not relieve your agency of its production obligation. Each request below that encompasses vendor-held records is made on this basis. Please retrieve and produce all responsive records held by your ALPR vendor(s) on your agency's behalf.

Note on record creation: I am seeking existing records only. I understand that no agency is required to create a new record in response to a PRA request. However, producing a screenshot or other faithful reproduction of an existing electronic display is not creating a new record — it is reproducing an existing one, in the same manner as photocopying or scanning a paper document. Agencies cannot evade their production obligation by characterizing screenshots of existing system displays as "new records."

I am requesting these records for research and public interest purposes, submitted through MuckRock, a nonprofit journalism platform. I request that your agency consider waiving any fees given that this request is submitted for research and public interest purposes and that electronic production of the requested records should entail minimal or no actual cost under RCW 42.56.120. For any anticipated costs, please notify me before incurring them so that I may prioritize or narrow the request. This notification requirement should not be construed as authorization to delay production of records for which no fee will be incurred pending resolution of fees for other records in this request

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Phil Mocek

This notification requirement should not be construed as authorization to delay production of records for which no fee will be incurred pending resolution of fees for other records in this request 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Phil Mocek 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2239215/” 
Your request will be forwarded to the applicable department(s) for response. 
You will be notified regarding the status of your request in 5 business days per RCW 42.56.520. 
 
Thank you for your submission, 
City of Auburn

communications.subject: Public Records Request :: P049101-031526
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of March 15, 2026, Reference # P049101-031526 Dear Phil : Pursuant to RCW 42.56.520 and Soter v. Cowles Pub. Co, this letter acknowledges that the City has received your above referenced public records request on 3/15/2026. Due to the number of requests the City is currently processing, and the time period needed to gather and review responsive records for potential exemptions, redactions and third party notices, the City estimates that it will take up to approximately 30 calendar -days from the date of this letter to respond to your request (either in full, or partially through a first installment of records). Please note the following helpful information as the City processes your records request: 
 
  *  The City responds to its numerous Public Records Requests in the order that they are received 
  *  The City may respond to larger records requests by providing responsive records in installments 
  *  The City's production of records to you may require you to pay fees and costs as allowed by law If your request seeks police-related records: these requests frequently generate large sets of responsive records. The City's practice is to respond by providing requested records in installments. First installments typically include responsive documents such as police reports, CAD incident reports, and photos. Later installments may include police in-car video or body camera footage. You will be provided a time estimate for the next installment as each installment becomes available. Narrowing the scope of your request may result in fewer installments, and therefore reduced overall response time. If you are interested in narrowing the scope of your request in any way, please respond to this message or contact our office at (253) 931-3039. If the City needs additional information to clarify your request, or to modify its estimated response date above, City staff will contact you. To view the full details of  this request please log into the Public Records Center.  Please note that this correspondence is for informational purposes only, and you are not required to respond. Katie Sorum 
Records Clerk 
City Clerk's Office 
253-931-3039

communications.subject: Public Records Request :: P049101-031526
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of March 15, 2026, Reference # P049101-031526 Dear Phil : The City of Auburn received a public records request from you on March 15, 2026. You requested the following: "To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

1. All system audit logs, as natively maintained in your agency's systems or your vendor's systems, reflecting access to your agency's ALPR system or any system used to store, process, or query ALPR data (e.g., Axon Evidence.com, Flock Safety portal, Motorola/Vigilant), by users affiliated with any agency other than your agency. 

2. All system audit logs, as natively maintained, reflecting the Originating Agency Identifier (ORI) or agency name associated with each external query of your agency's ALPR system. 

3. All system audit logs, as natively maintained, reflecting the user identifier, badge number, or account name associated with each query of your agency's ALPR system, whether the querying user was internal or external to your agency. 

4. All records identifying which external agencies are currently authorized to access, query, or receive data from your agency's ALPR system, including any configuration record, permissions list, access control list, or authorized-agency registry maintained in your ALPR platform. If this information exists only as a display within a software interface, a screenshot, faithful reproduction, or export of that display to any available file format is an acceptable and required form of production. 

Note on vendor-held records: In Rodriguez v. City of Stanwood & Sedro-Woolley (Skagit County Superior Court, Nov. 6, 2025), the court held that ALPR data held by a third-party SaaS vendor on behalf of a government agency is a public record subject to PRA disclosure. Vendor custody does not relieve your agency of its production obligation. Each request below that encompasses vendor-held records is made on this basis. Please retrieve and produce all responsive records held by your ALPR vendor(s) on your agency's behalf. 

Note on record creation: I am seeking existing records only. I understand that no agency is required to create a new record in response to a PRA request. However, producing a screenshot or other faithful reproduction of an existing electronic display is not creating a new record — it is reproducing an existing one, in the same manner as photocopying or scanning a paper document. Agencies cannot evade their production obligation by characterizing screenshots of existing system displays as "new records." 

I am requesting these records for research and public interest purposes, submitted through MuckRock, a nonprofit journalism platform. I request that your agency consider waiving any fees given that this request is submitted for research and public interest purposes and that electronic production of the requested records should entail minimal or no actual cost under RCW 42.56.120. For any anticipated costs, please notify me before incurring them so that I may prioritize or narrow the request. This notification requirement should not be construed as authorization to delay production of records for which no fee will be incurred pending resolution of fees for other records in this request 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Phil Mocek 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2239215/" The City of Auburn has located the following records responsive to your request: Audit logs from May 2023 - March 15, 2026, all records identifying access to and from external agencies CLICK HERE TO VIEW RECORD The City believes that it has fulfilled your request by: 
 Providing all known records responsive to your request Therefore, the City is officially closing this request as of 3/24/2026 and will provide no further responses to this request. If you believe the City has closed this request in error, have questions, or need clarification concerning this request, please respond to this e-mail within 30 calendar days from the closing date above. The Public Records Acts gives the requestor one year from the date a city closes a records request to file in court an allegation that the City violated the Act. For this records request, that opportunity expires one year from today. You may also petition the Public Records Officer in writing (including email) for review by the City Attorney of any denial of records. That opportunity expires in 30 days. Sincerely, Rebecca Wood Deputy City Clerk City Clerk's Office 253-398-7298

title: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (Northern Kentucky University)

communications.subject: Kentucky Open Records Act Request: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (Northern Kentucky University)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Kentucky Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Greetings,

I am looking to make a request for records related to your athletic department.

1) I would like a copy of the annual NCAA MFRS report for FY2017 - FY2025. These annual reports are submitted by the athletic department and record the revenue/expenses of the department. It is an approximately 90 page document listing revenue and expenses in the athletic department by team. It is a standard annual NCAA form. The latest FY2025 report was due, 1/15/2025 so should be available. If you are unable to include this, can you please specify if you will send it to me immediately when it's available or if you want me to make a new request. That should be 9 reports combined.

2) I'd like copies of the latest contracts for your athletic director and head coaches of football, mens/womens basketball, and mens/womens ice hockey if you have those teams. That should be 6 documents if you have that many teams.

Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for your time.

Best,

Greg Chick

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Greg Chick

communications.subject: FW: [EXT] Kentucky Open Records Act Request: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (Northern Kentucky University)
communications.communication:

This information is exempted from disclosure pursuant to KRS 61.878(1)(a) because the information is of a personal nature and the disclosure thereof would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy.
Regards,

Office of Legal Affairs and General Counsel
Northern Kentucky University
5320 Campus Drive
Lucas Administrative Center, Room 812
Phone: (859) 572-5587
openrecords@nku.edu<mailto:openrecords@nku.edu>

title: Records Pertaining to Colonel Kurush Bharucha-Reid (“KB Reid”) (United States Department of the Army)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Records Pertaining to Colonel Kurush Bharucha-Reid (“KB Reid”) (United States Department of the Army)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

This request seeks all records from 1995 (or earlier) to 2010 (or later if available) referencing Colonel Kurush Bharucha-Reid (also known as “KB Reid”), a U.S. Army Military Intelligence officer who was selected for the Military Intelligence Excepted Career Program (GREAT SKILL) in 1987 and subsequently served in some of the most sensitive Army and interagency HUMINT roles of the post–Cold War and post-9/11 era.

I respectfully request:

	1.	Personnel Records
	•	Official Military Personnel File (OMPF), Officer Evaluation Reports (OERs), commendations, promotions, awards, fitness reports, assignment orders, and internal performance evaluations for Colonel Kurush Bharucha-Reid (SSN/Service Number can be provided if required).
	•	Decorations including: Defense Superior Service Medal, Bronze Star (with Oak Leaf Cluster), Defense Meritorious Service Medal (x3), Meritorious Service Medal (x3), Joint Service Commendation Medal, Army Commendation Medal (with Oak Leaf Cluster), Army Achievement Medal, Ranger tab, Special Forces tab, Pathfinder, Master Parachutist, and Military Free Fall Jump Master badges ￼.
	2.	Program and Command Assignments
	•	All records documenting his service in the GREAT SKILL Program, including appointment, reassignment memos, or internal assessments.
	•	His service as Regional Desk Officer and Special Assistant for Military Affairs at a national-level intelligence agency, with a focus on East Asian and Near East operations.
	•	Records from his time as Commander of a Defense HUMINT Service base in Bosnia, and as CJ2 for Combined Joint Task Force Fervent Archer.
	•	Documentation from his command of a sensitive HUMINT collection detachment deployed to Afghanistan (2002), where he served as the first HUMINT case officer supporting U.S. Special Operations Command (USSOCOM).
	•	Records from his second Afghanistan deployment (2004), where he commanded a HUMINT base supporting surge operations during Afghanistan’s first democratic elections.
	•	Internal records, rosters, orders, or awards tied to his 2005 Iraq deployment as a Senior HUMINT Advisor in a sensitive interagency coordination cell.
	3.	Strategic Instruction & Final Command
	•	Records of his time as Chief of the Military Group and Senior DoD Instructor at a joint interagency strategic intelligence training institution (likely DIA or a multi-agency DoD school).
	•	Materials from his command of the U.S. Army Field Support Center (AFSC) beginning in May 2009, including speeches, directives, or internal comms.
	•	Any documentation of his death from pancreatic cancer in 2010, honors received, or correspondence related to his funeral and burial at Arlington National Cemetery on September 9, 2010.
	4.	Legacy Sites and Honors
	•	Records regarding the 2011 naming of the DIA Contingency Operating Base in Kabul as Camp K.B. Reid, including naming justification, interagency approvals, and dedication ceremony photos or memos.
	•	Materials related to the Reid Center (DIA’s training facility in Norfolk, VA, established in 2012 and now closed).
	•	The establishment of the COL Kurush Bharucha-Reid Award for Excellence in HUMINT Tradecraft in 2014 by the HUMINT Training – Joint Center of Excellence, including award criteria, selection board minutes, and awardee names.
	•	Any correspondence, ceremony documentation, or Hall of Fame entry details from his 2014 induction into the Military Intelligence Corps Hall of Fame ￼.
	5.	Photographic and Ceremonial Records
	•	All official photographs, eulogies, obituaries, citations, press releases, internal honors, or other agency acknowledgments related to Col. Bharucha-Reid, both during service and posthumously.
	6.	Classified and Segregable Records
	•	If any records are classified, please initiate a mandatory declassification review (MDR) under Executive Order 13526 and release all segregable non-exempt portions under FOIA guidelines.
	7.	Delivery and Fee Waiver
	•	Please provide documents in searchable PDF format via secure email or download link.

This request supports a public-interest historical and educational project—specifically, a feature film titled Undercover Patriot exploring the intersection of identity, secrecy, and service. 

As a fellow Parsi Zoroastrian American, I believe Col. Bharucha-Reid’s legacy deserves recognition beyond the classified corridors in which he served.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Adil Minocherhomjee

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all email communications sent and received by each school board chair in the district/SAU related to their position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on district or secondary accounts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

communications.subject: Re: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Monadnock Regional School District)
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

Please see attached.

Please note that any responsive records containing student educational
record information are exempt from disclosure under RSA 91-A:5, IV, which
protects confidential files and individual privacy. Furthermore, such
records are protected by the federal Family Educational Rights and Privacy
Act (FERPA), which prohibits the unauthorized release of personally
identifiable information from a student's education record.

Thank you,
Jeremy




-- 


Jeremy Rathbun

Superintendent of Schools

SAU93 - Monadnock Regional School District

p:

(603)903-6977  m: (603)903-3736

a:

600 Old Homestead Highway, Swanzey, NH 03446

This email and any files transmitted with it are confidential and intended
solely for the use of the individual or entity to whom they are addressed.
If you have received this email in error, please notify the system manager.
This message contains confidential information and is intended only for the
individual named. If you are not the named addressee you should not
disseminate, distribute or copy this e-mail. Please notify the sender
immediately by e-mail if you have received this e-mail by mistake and
delete this e-mail from your system. If you are not the intended recipient,
you are notified that disclosing, copying, distributing or taking any
action in reliance on the contents of this information is strictly
prohibited.  The Right-To-Know Law provides that most e-mail communications
to or from School District employees regarding the business of the School
District are government records available to the public upon request.
Therefore, this e-mail communication may be subject to public disclosure.

title: Flock Safety search audits, network sharing, and general order

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Flock Safety search audits, network sharing, and general order
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Documents sufficient to show all user accounts for the department’s Flock deployment that were active between 3/29/2023 and the date that this request is processed.

Documents that show who has access to the department’s Flock deployment and the access that is enabled/disabled for all user accounts (current and previously suspended, revoked, terminated, dormant, etc.). The report should include all available details including, but not limited to, the following where available: User ID, First Name, Last Name, Title, Role, Status, Identity Provider, Last login, User Mgmt, Search, Device Mgmt, Hotlist Tool.

Records related to the department’s Flock Safety system:
2. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of March 29, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

3. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of March 29, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

4. A list of network share settings that include:

4a. Networks that I’m sharing -
The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares its Flock Safety ALPR data, also referred to as "Networks that I’m sharing."
4b. Flock networks shared with me -
The names of agencies and organizations that have shared their cameras with the Agency, also referred to as "Flock networks shared with me."

5. Any Non‑email records covering Flock Safety training presentations, published procedures,  directives, training materials, promotional materials, or legal guidance.



Please provide all responsive records in their original electronic and machine readable format. This includes, but is not limited to, spreadsheet files such as CSV or Excel, slide presentation files such as PowerPoint, and any other original native file formats. Please do not substitute scanned images or PDFs for original files, and please do not direct me to your transparency portal in place of providing the records.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Hank

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Randolph County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Randolph County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: FOIA for who is the Thurston County Sheriff, Derek Sanders (Records & Public Disclosure Section - Thurston County)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: FOIA for who is the Thurston County Sheriff, Derek Sanders (Records & Public Disclosure Section - Thurston County)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

This is a request under the Freedom of Information Act, The Sunshine Act 5 U.S.C. § 552b, and Washington Public Records Act Title 14 Chapter 44-14 and related. 

Please provide the records in electronic form, and produce them on a rolling basis as they become available for  Thurston County Sheriff, Derek Sanders.

If a record exists but cannot be produced, please state the reason and provide any non-exempt portions. If the record does not exist or is not held by your agency, please confirm which of these applies.

●A copy of their Public Official surety bond that is required by Washington Title 28A.330.060
● A copy of the blanket surety bond
● A copy of their Errors & Omissions (E&O), a Surety Liability Insurance policy, and the Duty of Care policy if applicable.
● A copy of their General Obligation Bonds if applicable.
● A copy of their general long-term bond if applicable.
● A copy of their Crime Policy if applicable.
● A copy of their Risk Management Policy if applicable.
● A copy of the following documents if applicable:
○ ACORD 125
○ ACORD 126
○ ACORD 127
○ ACORD 128
○ The documents should indicate the policy number and the insured amount of the policy.
● Certificate of Liability if applicable.
● Public Officials and/or any other bonds pertaining to proof of liability and policies.
Based on any and all losses of financial responsibility due to negligence or dishonesty.
Any and all based on the contract of terms and conditions.
● A copy of their faithful Performance Bond if applicable.
● A copy of their Fidelity Bond if applicable.
● A copy of their Public Employee Dishonesty Policy if applicable.
● A copy of their Public Employee Blanket Bond if applicable.
● A copy of their Statutory Bond if applicable.
● A copy of the power of attorney for the surety bond company.
● A copy of the Blanket Bond power of attorney for the surety bond company if applicable.
● A copy of their oath of office.
● A copy of their Anti Bribery and Corruption Statement.
● A copy of their Form NDS-1, Registration Statement Pursuant to the Foreign Agents Registration Act.

I am seeking information concerning their public official surety bond, or the Errors & Omissions (E&O), and or the Duty of Care policy that you are required by the state of Washington to obtain before swearing the oath of office.
If any document listed cannot be provided, please respond specifying which document(s) cannot be provided and the reason for each non-disclosure.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Anne Sackville

Anne Sackville

We have begun a process to search for records responsive to your request. We reasonably believe that at least a first installment of public records (if any) responsive to this request will be available on or before March 27, 2026. 
Additionally, please note that some of the records you are seeking such as Oaths are considered court records and maintained within the official court file held by Thurston County Superior Court. Court records are provided to the public pursuant to GR 31, rather than the Public Records Act; therefore, you will want to submit a separate request (if you have not already done so) to the court directly. Information about how to request records from the court file is available on their website at this link: https://www.thurstoncountywa.gov/departments/county-clerk/obtaining-documents 

If public records become available in advance of this estimate, we will contact you promptly. Conversely, if the County encounters a need to extend our estimate, we will contact you with a revised estimated date. We will communicate with you and keep you informed of any updates regarding collecting and obtaining the responsive records as we move through the process. 

RCW 42.56.120 provides that the County may charge fees as authorized by law for responsive public records documents. 
If you have any questions you may contact me at 360-754-4998. 

Sincerely, 

Sara Meath 
Paralegal 
Prosecutor's Office

communications.subject: [Records Center] Public Records Request :: P039037-121525
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

March 24, 2026 

Anne  Sackville 
MuckRock News, DEPT MR199501 263 Huntington Ave 
Boston, MA 02115 

Dear Anne Sackville: 

This communication is regarding your public records request, #P039037-121525. 
The records responsive to your request are available for review and download via your account on the Public Records Center. 
If you experience any difficulty viewing the records, please disable any pop-up blockers on your internet browser, as these may block your access to the records. 
We have made no redactions nor withheld any records in response to this request. 
As we mentioned previously, you may wish to contact the Thurston County Superior Court Clerk's Office for additional records of interest they may maintain. Additionally, some records, such as Oath documents may be considered recorded documents filed with the Thurston County Auditor's Office. These records do not fall within a public records request and are provided to the public through a different legal process than the Public Records Act, chapter 42.56 RCW. Information about how to request records from these departments may be found on the County's website at the links below: 
Thurston County Clerk's Office: https://www.thurstoncountywa.gov/departments/county-clerk/info/obtaining-copies-documents-filed-thurston-county-superior-court 
Recorded documents: https://www.thurstoncountywa.gov/departments/auditor/recording-services 
This is the final and definitive response to your records request, and your request is now closed. Please let us know at your earliest convenience if you believe we have misunderstood your records request or have omitted records that you believe your request covered. 
Please note that the PRA's one-year statute of limitations to seek judicial review has started to run because the County considers your request to have been fulfilled and does not intend to further respond to this request.  Should you wish to do so, you may also seek review pursuant to Thurston County Code 2.130.020. 

If you have any questions, you may contact me at 360-754-4998. 

Sincerely, 

Sara Meath 
Paralegal 
Prosecutor's Office

title: City of Santa Clarita: Photographs of Graffiti Uploaded Via Resident Service Center portion of city website

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: City of Santa Clarita: Photographs of Graffiti Uploaded Via Resident Service Center portion of city website
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All photographs of graffiti uploaded via the "Resident Service Center" option that is accessible via the city's website.

Santa Clarita's graffiti abatement efforts are discussed online here: https://santaclarita.gov/communityservices/graffiti-task-force/three-prong-approach/

Citizens are encourage to uploaded photographs of graffiti using the "Resident Service Center" which can be found here: https://user.govoutreach.com/santaclarita/faq.php?cmd=shell

Time Frame for request: June 1 2020 to March 1st 2026. 

This is a request for just the photographs. This is not a request for any other identifying information.

Please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed. There is a public interest in seeing what has been flagged as graffiti by the public to the city of Santa Clarita. 

Thank you to the city Santa Clarita for the commitment to transparency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (Virginia Military Institute)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Greetings,

I am looking to make a request for records related to your athletic department.

1) I would like a copy of the annual NCAA MFRS report for FY2017 - FY2025. These annual reports are submitted by the athletic department and record the revenue/expenses of the department. It is an approximately 90 page document listing revenue and expenses in the athletic department by team. It is a standard annual NCAA form. The latest FY2025 report was due, 1/15/2025 so should be available. If you are unable to include this, can you please specify if you will send it to me immediately when it's available or if you want me to make a new request. That should be 9 reports combined.

2) I'd like copies of the latest contracts for your athletic director and head coaches of football, mens/womens basketball, and mens/womens ice hockey if you have those teams. That should be 6 documents if you have that many teams.

Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for your time.

Best,

Greg Chick

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Greg Chick.
