title: Florida Public Records Request: FACES Facial Recognition System (Okaloosa County Sheriff's Department)

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Florida Public Records Request: FACES Facial Recognition System (Okaloosa County Sheriff's Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Public Records Law (Chapter 119, Florida Statutes), I hereby request the following records related to Okaloosa County Sheriffs Department's use of and access to the Pinellas County Sheriff's Office Face Analysis Comparison & Examination System (FACES) and related surveillance technologies:

1. FACES SYSTEM ACCESS POLICIES AND PROCEDURES:
   - All current written policies governing the department's use of the FACES facial recognition system
   - All memoranda of understanding (MOUs), memoranda of agreement (MOAs), interagency agreements, or contracts with Pinellas County Sheriff's Office or any other entity related to FACES access
   - Training materials provided to personnel authorized to use FACES
   - Standard operating procedures (SOPs) for conducting FACES searches
   - Documents describing civil rights protections, limitations on use, or oversight mechanisms for FACES searches

2. ADMINISTRATIVE LOGS AND USAGE RECORDS:
   - Organization Audit reports from FACES showing searches conducted by department personnel for the period of January 1st, 2026 to the date this request is processed if available
   - Documents sufficient to show:
     * Total number of FACES searches conducted by the department
     * User logs identifying which personnel conducted searches (names may be redacted if required by law, but please include badge numbers or employee IDs)
     * Case numbers or incident reports associated with each search
     * Stated reasons or justifications for each search
     * Any internal audit reports reviewing FACES usage for compliance or misuse

3. INTEGRATION AND RELATED TECHNOLOGIES:
   - All contracts, agreements, purchase orders, invoices, or correspondence with FUSUS/Axon or any entity related to FUSUS system implementation or integration
   - Documents describing any integration between FACES and the FUSUS platform or any other real-time surveillance systems
   - Technical specifications or system architecture documents showing how FACES connects or shares data with other surveillance technologies including but not limited to:
     * FUSUS real-time camera integration platform
     * Automated License Plate Readers (ALPRs)
     * Predictive policing systems
     * Body-worn cameras
     * ShotSpotter or other gunshot detection systems
     * Private camera registries
   - Any assessments, reports, or internal communications discussing the integration of FACES with other surveillance technologies
   - Documents describing any real-time crime center operations that utilize FACES

4. PERFORMANCE AND ACCURACY:
   - Any internal assessments, audits, or external reports evaluating the accuracy, effectiveness, or reliability of FACES
   - Documents addressing known error rates, particularly regarding demographic disparities
   - Any false positive reports or case files where FACES identification was determined to be incorrect

5. POLICY UPDATES AND OVERSIGHT:
   - Communications with Pinellas County Sheriff's Office regarding policy updates or requirements implemented in 2024
   - Documents related to any citizen oversight, internal affairs reviews, or complaints regarding FACES usage
   - Any reports provided to elected officials, oversight bodies, or the public regarding FACES usage

================================================================================

IMPORTANT CLARIFICATION REGARDING SCOPE OF REQUEST:

This request does NOT seek:
- Personally identifiable information (PII) of individuals who have been subjects of searches or investigations, including names, addresses, dates of birth, social security numbers, or biometric data of searched individuals
- Photographs, facial recognition images, or biometric templates of any individuals in the FACES database
- Specific investigative techniques, methodologies, or operational tactics that would compromise ongoing or future investigations
- Information that would reveal the identity of confidential informants or undercover officers

This request DOES seek:
- Policies, procedures, and governance documents regarding system use
- Aggregate statistical data and anonymized audit logs showing usage patterns, search volume, and compliance
- System integration information at an architectural level (what systems connect to what) without revealing specific security vulnerabilities
- Public accountability records such as oversight reports, accuracy assessments, and policy compliance reviews

I am seeking information about the governance, oversight, financial arrangements, and operational policies surrounding these technologies—not information about specific individuals, active investigations, or sensitive law enforcement techniques. All personal identifying information of subjects, victims, witnesses, or confidential sources should be redacted as required by law.

If any responsive records contain both exempt and non-exempt information, I request that you provide the non-exempt portions with appropriate redactions, as required by Florida Statutes § 119.07(1)(d).

================================================================================

FORMAT AND FEE WAIVER REQUEST:

Please provide the requested records in their original electronic, machine-readable format. For audit reports and logs, please provide data in .csv, .xlsx, or another database format if available. For policy documents, PDF format is acceptable.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. This request is made in the public interest to promote transparency and accountability regarding the use of facial recognition technology and surveillance systems. Therefore, I request a waiver of all fees associated with this request. If any fees apply, please inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request.

================================================================================

TIME FRAME:

I prefer to receive records electronically by email attachment. Florida law requires a response to this request within a reasonable time, the timeline for this request is 10 business days. If you anticipate difficulty meeting this timeline, please contact me.

If any portion of this request is denied, please:
1. Cite the specific statutory exemption being claimed for each withheld record
2. Provide a detailed explanation of why the exemption applies to the specific information being withheld
3. Indicate whether a redacted version of the record can be provided

Thank you for your assistance. Please contact me if you have any questions about this request or require clarification on any aspect of what is being sought.

Sincerely,

Kyle Searfoss

communications.subject: RE: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Florida Public Records Request: FACES Facial Recognition System (Okaloosa County Sheriff's Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I am writing to appeal the partial denial of my public records request regarding technology/system documentation. I respectfully contend that the information I am requesting does not fall within the scope of the exemptions cited in your response. Additionally, I am deeply disappointed in the fact that your department did not disclose that certain sections would be redacted/exempt prior to charging me a processing fee. 

Your response withheld Sections 2 and 3 entirely under F.S. 119.071(2)(c)(1) and F.S. 119.071(2)(d). However, the nature of the information I am requesting does not meet the criteria for these exemptions.

F.S. 119.071(2)(c)(1) - Criminal Investigative Information:

This exemption protects "active criminal intelligence information and active criminal investigative information." The records I requested are:
- Procurement and administrative documents showing which systems integrate with FACES to include systems like FUSUS

These are administrative and procurement records, not investigative records related to specific criminal cases or intelligence operations. The information exists independently of any particular investigation and pertains to how the department has configured its technology infrastructure, not how that technology is used in specific investigations.

Example: Knowing that FUSUS integrates with body-worn cameras, ALPR systems, FACES, or CAD systems does not reveal any information about active criminal investigations. This is administrative information about technology procurement and configuration.

F.S. 119.071(2)(d) - Surveillance Techniques or Procedures:

This exemption protects information "revealing surveillance techniques or procedures." The records I requested are:
- What systems connect to FACES and/or FUSUS(not how they are used operationally)
- Hardware specifications and equipment lists (not deployment strategies)

Hardware Redactions in Pileum Contract:

The unsigned Pileum(FUSUS) contract contains redactions over hardware specifications. Hardware information does not fall within either cited exemption:
- Hardware models and equipment specifications are not "criminal investigative information", they are procurement information.
- Equipment lists do not reveal "surveillance techniques", they identify what was purchased not how it is deployed.

Additional Matters:

Unsigned Contract: The Pileum(FUSUS) contract provided appears to be unsigned. Please clarify whether an executed agreement exists and provide the signed version.

Duplicate MOU: The Pinellas County MOU appears to have been provided twice under different names. Please clarify if these are identical documents.

Conclusion:

The information I am requesting is administrative in nature and does not meet the statutory definitions of the exemptions you have cited. I am not seeking:
- Details of active criminal investigations
- Operational procedures or tactical deployments
- Security vulnerabilities or system access credentials
- Specific surveillance methodologies

I request that you:
1. Reconsider the applicability of the cited exemptions to the requested administrative and procurement information.
2. Provide the requested records, or at minimum, provide all segregable portions.
3. Provide unredacted hardware specifications from the FUSUS contract.
4. Provide specific, detailed justification if you maintain these exemptions apply.

Thank you for your reconsideration of this matter.

Sincerely,

Kyle Searfoss

communications.subject: Fw: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Florida Public Records Request: FACES Facial Recognition System (Okaloosa County Sheriff's Department)

Upon review of your requests as well as the records, the determination that the records are confidential and exempt pursuant to the two cited statutory provisions was correct. As the records are confidential and exempt from public disclosure, they will not be released pursuant to a public records request.

title: Upshur County, WV Opioid Settlememt Funds Spring 2026

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Upshur County, WV Opioid Settlememt Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Samantha Smith
1511 University Avenue, P.O. Box 6010
Morgantown, WV 26506-6010
March 12, 2026

Carol J. Smith, County Clerk
40 West Main Street, Room 101
Buckhannon, WV 26201 

To Whom It May Concern:

Under the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, §29-B-1-1 et seq., I am requesting an opportunity to inspect or obtain copies of public records that pertain to Upshur County opioid settlement funds. Specifically, I am requesting: 
- Documentation of the amount of funds the county has received from the federal opioid lawsuit settlements from March 1, 2025 to March 12, 2026.
- The application forms that any organization would submit to receive settlement funds
- Documentation of how much county-level funding has been awarded from March 1, 2025 to March 12, 2026.
- Any completed application of an organization that has received settlement funds from the county from from March 1, 2025 to March 12, 2026.
- Documentation of the process by which funds are awarded
- Any documents that contain the names of the individuals who review the funding applications and determine the awards
- Any documents that account for future plans from the county for the use of opioid settlement funds
If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me if the cost will exceed $25. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of how the opioid settlement funds are being spent. I am a student journalist whose request is strictly for news-gathering purposes. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes. 
The West Virginia Freedom of Information Act requires a response to this request be made within five business days. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer than this amount of time, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records.


If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law. 
Thank you, Samantha Smith
samanthamsmithmedia@gmail.com

files.title: Fund 040 - March 2025 through March 2026
files.source: Upshur County Clerk Office, Upshur County, WV

communications.communication:
In response to your FOIA request dated, March 12, 2026 and received by our office on March 19, 2026, I have compiled the following information.  Between the time period of March 1, 2025 through March 12, 2026, the Upshur County Commission received $59,420.16 from the WV Opioid Qualified Settlement Fund.  These funds were deposited into the opioid Settlement Fund which had a remaining balance of $150,360.49 as of March 1, 2025.  
The Upshur County Commission has not outlined a separate process for requesting opioid funds. Therefore, when an entity desires to request funding, correspondence is submitted to the County Commission and then placed on a Commission Meeting agenda for consideration. All three County Commissioners (Samuel R. Nolte, Kristie G. Tenney and Douglas K. Bush) review the request. A motion may be made to move forward with a request or in the alternative, no action may be taken. A motion requires two affirmative votes to pass. 
For expenditures incurred by the local government, each County Commission Meeting agenda published contains an item for discussion/action/approval for approving invoices for payment, purchase card invoices for payment, and financial reports. Therefore, County expenditures are reviewed during County Commission meetings and initialed by at least two County Commissioners prior to remitting payment. The County Clerk processes the invoices and prepares the checks which require three signatures. 
For larger, non-immediate purchases, those are considered during budget hearings. Budget hearings are conducted every March to assess revenue and expenditures for the upcoming fiscal year along with needs from each Elected Officials office. All three Commissioners participate in the budget hearings, which are duly noticed. The proposed budget is then approved by the levying body during a regularly scheduled Commission Meeting. During the budget hearings conducted in March 2025, the Commission identified the need to continue utilizing opioid settlement funds for a Drug Task Force employee and regional jail expenses. Therefore, during a regularly scheduled Commission Meeting held on June 26, 2025, the Commission approved a resolution allowing $101,701.25 to be used for reimbursement of expenditures made by the County for regional jail fees.
At times, there are unforeseen needs that arise which are considered throughout the year. Those requests are made by the elected official to the Commission and are considered on a case-by-case basis. Additional funds that have been spent were for law enforcement equipment to curtail the sale, distribution, promotion and use of opioids and other drugs. That equipment includes: 
•	Partial payment of a 2025 Can-Am Defender = $13,500 (discussed during the March 13, 2025 Commission Meeting, approved and paid March 21, 2025)
In addition to the above appropriations, the Commission employs a member of the Drug Task Force and therefore is reimbursed for those wages from the opioid fund as his work is targeted to curtail the sale, distribution, promotion and use of opioids and other drugs. During the above referenced time period, $59,289.02 have been spent for this purpose.
As of March 23, 2026, there are $35,290.38 remaining of unexpended settlement funds. The County Commission conducted budget hearings earlier in March 2026 for the new fiscal year beginning July 1. Without a projected amount of settlement funds to be received during the upcoming fiscal year, future plans for the use of opioid settlement funds remains undetermined and will be discussed upon receipt.
  For additional information, approved County Commission Meeting minutes are accessible at https://www.upshurcounty.org/agenda_and_minutes/index.php. If you have questions relating to the information provided, please feel free to contact our office. 
Best regards,


Tabatha R. Perry
County Administrator
Enclosures: as referenced above

title: AI policy (Rochester Clerk)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: AI policy (Rochester Clerk)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of this department’s policies that guide the use of Artificial Intelligence, including any best practices guidance, software restrictions, privacy policies, and other materials that prohibit, encourage or outline rules on the use of both certain artificial intelligence systems and AI generally. If the department has conducted any internal or external studies on the use of AI, please provide a copy of that as well.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kelly Kauffman

communications.subject: RE: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: AI policy (Rochester Clerk)
communications.communication:
Hello Kelly,

Please accept this email as acknowledgment of the receipt of your right-to-know request pursuant to RSA 91-A regarding City of Rochester AI policies.


Pursuant to McBurney v. Young, U.S. Supreme Court, No. 12-17, 4/29/13, we do not have an obligation to send records out of state (electronically or otherwise).  However, we will make records available, should they exist, for public inspection on site during regular business hours.



If you would like to set up a time to view the requested records, if any such records exist, please reply to this email.

communications.subject: RE: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: AI policy (Rochester Clerk)
communications.communication:
Thank you for the response.  We hope to receive records electronically instead of on paper to eliminate cost and waste. New Hampshire courts have consistently ruled that any record stored in an electronic format should be produced in that manner. In addition, the NH Department of Justice makes clear that “public bodies and public agencies are to maintain their public records in a way that makes them available to the public.” 

If this record is stored electronically, we would appreciate receiving it via email. Given the limited scope of this request, is it possible to provide these records electronically? Many NH agencies routinely fulfill requests via email, and requiring an in-person visit creates a significant barrier to access. If electronic delivery is not possible, would you consider mailing copies? We are happy to prepay copying and postage costs.

Regarding state residency: while some agencies interpret the term "citizen" narrowly, the New Hampshire Supreme Court and the Right-to-Know Ombudsman have consistently emphasized that the statute’s purpose is to ensure the "greatest possible public access." As a journalist working on a project specifically focused on New Hampshire public record transparency, I would ask the agency to reconsider these blanket denials. That said, Jonathan Van Fleet of the Concord Monitor, a New Hampshire resident, and the Granite State News Collaborative, a New Hampshire-based media organization, are collaborators on the project this request is supporting, have been added to this request, and should be considered co-requestors. 

Please let us know if you have additional questions about this request, and we appreciate your help. 

Sincerely,
Kelly Kauffman and and Jonathan Van Fleet

title: James W. McCord Jr.

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: James W. McCord Jr.
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records related to James Walter McCord Jr. (died June 15, 2017). Mr. McCord served as an FBI special agent and was one of the Watergate burglars. 

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records for all relevant names, individuals, agencies and companies at FBI Headquarters. Please also conduct a search for records at the Alexandria Records Center. If any potentially responsive records have been destroyed and/or transferred to NARA, then I request copies of the destruction or transfer slips as well as any other documentation relating to, mentioning or describing said transfer or destruction, to include but not be limited to confirmation that the Bureau has no other copies of said records. If previously-released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

REQUEST FOR FEE WAIVER

I request that all fees related to the processing of this request be waived. As one of the Watergate burglars, James McCord was a key player in a significant moment in American history. And because Mr. McCord was an FBI employee, the Bureau's records are likely to provide the public with significant insight into that moment. I am not making this request for commercial purposes— I am making it so that responsive records will be easily available to students, historians, journalists, and the general public. All responsive documents will be made available to the general public online for free. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges before fulfilling my request.

I would prefer that this request be filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or by CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Nicholas Marritz

correspondence will be forthcoming.

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #1437109-000
communications.communication:
Re: FOIPA Request No.: 1437109-000

Dear FBI:

In response to my above-numbered FOIA request for records relating to James Walter McCord, Jr., I received the following response:

"In an effort to provide you with responsive records as expeditiously as possible, we are releasing documents from previous requests regarding your subject. We consider your request fulfilled. Since we relied on previous results, additional records potentially responsive to your subject may exist. If this release of previously processed material does
not satisfy your request, you may request an additional search for records."  

I hereby request an additional search for records. Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records for all relevant names, individuals, agencies and companies at FBI Headquarters. Please also conduct a search for records at the Alexandria Records Center. If any potentially responsive records have been destroyed and/or transferred to NARA, then I request copies of the destruction or transfer slips as well as any other documentation relating to, mentioning or describing said transfer or destruction, to include but not be limited to confirmation that the Bureau has no other copies of said records. If previously-released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

Thank you very much.

Most sincerely,

Nicholas Marritz

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL EMAIL] -  RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #1437109-000
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

Thank you for contacting foipaquestions@fbi.gov.  Please check the status of your FOIPA Request at http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on “Check Status of Your FOI/PA Request” on the right side of the page, and follow the instructions below.

Check the Status of Your FOIPA Request
If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-0] please enter [1234567-000] into the system. If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-1] please enter [1234567-001] into the system. If you have any questions about the status of your FOIPA request, please e-mail foipaquestions@fbi.gov.

FIND STATUS OF FOIPA- Request statuses are updated weekly

Please enter the whole FOIPA number-Example: [1234567-000]

FOIPA:
Results will show the Request Number, Case Type and Process Description shown below:
FOIPA:
1234567-000

Case Type:
FOIPA

Process Description (Will display the current progress of the request)
The FBI’s FOIPA Program is searching the FBI’s indices for potentially responsive documents.
You may be contacted via formal letter for all fees and/or negotiation issues that may apply.

NOTE: Recent requests are entered into the FOIPA database in the order that they are received. Before you can check the status, you must have received correspondence assigning a FOIPA request number and the information transferred to the online database. Status information is updated weekly. If a request has been closed within the last six months the online database will display the following: The FOIPA number entered has been closed, and appropriate correspondence has been sent to the address on file.

Estimated Dates of Completion
Requests are processed in the order in which they are received through our multi-track processing system.  Requests are divided into two primary tracks--simple (under 50 pages of potentially responsive documents) and complex (over 50 pages of potentially responsive documents).  Complex requests are further divided into medium, large, and extra-large sub-tracks based upon request size.  Simple track requests typically require the least amount of time to process.  Currently, simple track cases average approximately 152 days from the date of receipt for processing.  Our complex requests in the medium processing track are currently averaging 922 days, large processing track are currently averaging approximately 1,964 days, and extra-large processing track are currently averaging 2,360 days for processing.

Respectfully,

Public Information Officer
FBI - Information Management Division
200 Constitution Drive
Winchester, VA  22602
O: (540) 868-4593
E: foipaquestions@fbi.gov<mailto:foipaquestions@fbi.gov>

Do you have further questions about the FOI/PA process? Visit us at http://www.fbi.gov/foia

Please check the status of your request online at https://vault.fbi.gov/fdps-1/@@search-fdps    Status updates are performed on a weekly basis.

Note:  This is a non-emergency email address.  If this is an emergency, please call 911 directly.  If you need to report a tip for immediate action, please contact FBI Tips at http://tips.fbi.gov/ or reach out to your local field office.

title: FOIA request N. Taitano - Army

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA request N. Taitano - Army
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Official Military Personnel File (OMPF) for Norman Taitano
Name of Veteran: Norman Taitano
Branch: United States Army
DOB: November 14, 1944
Dates of Service: 1964 to 1987

Please provide the following military service information:
1. dates of service, dates of ranks, stations, list of awards, decorations, Badges, campaigns, assignments, Vietnam War service dates
2. Section VII CURRENT AND PREVIOUS ASSIGNMENTS form
3. copy of Bronze Star Medal award citation
4. Military Education/ training courses, military schools
5. request copy of Defense Meritorious Service Medal award citation
6. request copy of Army commendation award citations
7  request copy of Joint Meritorious Unit Award citation
8. Section V MISCELLANEOUS Form
9. date of discharge
10: Overseas service
I request copies of paperwork in the file showing this information

First name: Norman 
Middle initial: C
Last name: Taitano
DOB 11/14/1944

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eric Bradley

title: Flock Safety ALPR Audits

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Flock Safety ALPR Audits
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of February 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of February 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

Please provide the audit data in a structured format such as .csv, .xls, or another machine-readable database filetype.

3. Data Sharing from the period of February 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. 
a. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares ALPR data
b. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives ALPR data
c. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares “hot list” information
d. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives “hot list” information

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Concerned

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Flock Safety search audits (San Jose Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records related to the department’s Flock Safety system:
1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of March 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of March 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. A list of network share settings that include:
3a. Flock networks shared with me
3b. Networks that I’m sharing

Please provide records in their original electronic, machine readable format. For example, please provide csv or spreadsheet files where applicable. Please do not redirect me to your transparency portal.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Gerardo Leon

communications.subject: CUSTOMER - New Customer Welcome Message

communications.communication:
Dear Gerardo Leon: 
Thank you for your interest in public records of the San Jose Police Department. Your request was received on 1/14/2026 and is being processed. The reference number for tracking purposes is P007257-011426 . Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Records related to the department’s Flock Safety system: 
1. Organization Audit in Flock. 
The report should include data logged from the period of March 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency. 

2. Network Audit in Flock. 
The report should include data logged from the period of March 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System. 

3. A list of network share settings that include: 
3a. Flock networks shared with me 
3b. Networks that I’m sharing 

Please provide records in their original electronic, machine readable format. For example, please provide csv or spreadsheet files where applicable. Please do not redirect me to your transparency portal. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Gerardo Leon

title: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (secretary of state)

communications.subject: New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (secretary of state)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

From January 20, 2025 to the present:

1. All correspondence, emails, letters, and written communications between the agency and the U.S. Department of Justice, including but not limited to the Civil Rights Division, the Office of the Attorney General, and the Executive Office for Immigration Review, relating to voter registration data, voter files, voter rolls, or voter list maintenance.

2. All internal communications within the agency (including emails, memoranda, and briefing materials) discussing, referencing, or responsive to any DOJ request for voter registration data or any proposed or executed memorandum of understanding with the DOJ regarding voter data.

3. Any lists, spreadsheets, databases, or other records received from the DOJ identifying specific voters for removal, investigation, or further review.

4. All records relating to the engagement of any third-party contractor in connection with DOJ voter data requests or voter list maintenance activities conducted pursuant to any agreement with the DOJ.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

* All documents and records pertaining to syllabi for Math courses at the University from the last two (2) years.
* All documents and records pertaining to syllabi for Statistics courses at the University from the last two (2) years.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Sidhanth Grover

communications.subject: FW: [External] California Public Records Act Request: Math and Statistics courses syllabi (California State University Fullerton)
communications.communication:
Dear Sidanth Grover:
We received your November 30, 2025 Public Records Act request for the following records:
Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

·       All documents and records pertaining to syllabi for Math courses at the University from the last two (2) years.

·       All documents and records pertaining to syllabi for Statistics courses at the University from the last two (2) years.
CSU has conducted a reasonable search for these records under California Government Code §7922.535 and has determined that your request, either in whole or in part, seeks copies of disclosable public records. CSU will make available to you any that are public. Records subject to the following exemptions will be withheld or redacted as appropriate:

  *   Employee records that include personal information, the release of which would constitute an invasion of personal privacy. Cal. Govt. Code §§7927.700, 7928.300, 7922.000.
  *   General Privacy Rights. Cal. Govt. Code §7927.705; Cal. Const. Art. 1, §1.We will let you know when we have gathered all the records.
It will be necessary to redact from them any personal and otherwise privileged information before they can be made available to you. CSU estimates the records will be available January 29, 2026.
If you wish to inspect the records at Cal State Fullerton, you may contact me to schedule a mutually convenient date and time. If you wish to receive copies instead, CSU may charge the direct cost of duplication (20 cents per page). Once the records are located, we will advise you as to the exact number of pages upon which any charge will be based. We will require full payment before we begin the redaction and duplication process. The decision that certain records are exempt and will not be disclosed was made by the Records Access Officer at Cal State Fullerton, Anne Grogan.
Sincerely,
Anne Grogan, ARM (she/her)
Compliance Manager
Risk Management & Compliance
Human Resources and Inclusive Excellence
California State University, Fullerton
P: 657-278-5465

[cid:image001.png@01DC6532.6E4D4480]

communications.subject: RE: [External] California Public Records Act Request: Math and Statistics courses syllabi (California State University Fullerton)
communications.communication:
Dear Sidhanth Grover:
On November 30, 2025, we received a request under the California Public Records Act (“CPRA”) for the following records:

·         All documents and records pertaining to syllabi for Math courses at the University from the last two (2) years.

·         All documents and records pertaining to syllabi for Statistics courses at the University from the last two (2) years.
The request is extremely broad, and CSU would like to assist you in making a focused and effective request that reasonably describes identifiable records as required by Government Code section 7922.600.
The search has resulted in more than 17 MB (1200 files, 397 folders) of data, all of which must be carefully and individually reviewed to determine whether it is subject to disclosure under the CPRA. If exemptions apply, the record must be marked for withholding, disclosure, and/or redaction. A secondary review is conducted before redactions are applied, and the records can then be released. This process is extremely time-consuming, and we estimate it will take at least four months to complete.
Such an exhaustive search is not warranted, particularly when it is difficult to discern the public interest involved in the myriad of records that would be responsive to an overly broad and unfocused request. By contrast, the public interest served by not conducting a time-consuming fishing expedition for potentially responsive public records is readily apparent.
Additionally, many of the records that you seek are exempt from production and would be withheld or subject to heavy redaction.

  *   Employee records that include personal information, the release of which would constitute an invasion of personal privacy. Cal. Govt. Code §§7927.700, 7928.300, 7922.000.
  *   General Privacy Rights. Cal. Govt. Code §7927.705; Cal. Const. Art. 1, §1.
Because the search, as currently worded, would result in the review of a high volume of records under the CPRA, the public interest served by conducting the search is minimal and strongly outweighed by the public interest served by not conducting the search (protecting student/employee privacy rights, guarding against the waste of state resources). Govt. Code § 7922.000.
To assist you in making a focused and effective request that reasonably describes identifiable records, CSU provides the following information: Many instructors teach the same class multiple times during the semester, where the syllabi are almost identical to one another, with the exception of the course number.
I propose narrowing the request to one syllabus from each instructor for each class taught during the semester. Additionally, I suggest that we narrow the request to syllabi from Fall 2024 to Fall 2025. If these recommendations do not meet your needs, I invite you to engage in a dialogue to better understand the records you are seeking. Please contact me to schedule a date and time for a phone call.
The decision that certain records are exempt and will not be disclosed was made by the Records Access Officer at Cal State Fullerton, Anne Grogan.
Sincerely,

Anne Grogan, ARM (she/her)
Compliance Manager
Risk Management & Compliance
Human Resources and Inclusive Excellence
California State University, Fullerton
P: 657-278-5465

[cid:image002.png@01DC70DD.11220DC0]

Sincerely,

Maxine Doogan

communications.subject: Re: FOIA #06336093 Extension
communications.communication:
February 24, 2026


Re: FOIA #06336093 Extension

The Illinois State Police (ISP) acknowledges receipt of your attached Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request. A response to your request is due February 24, 2026. However, additional time is needed to respond to your request. In accordance with Section 3(e) of FOIA (5 ILCS 140/3(e)), ISP is extending the response due date for an additional five business days for the following reasons:

The requested records are stored in whole or in part at another location.

The request requires the collection of a substantial number of records.

The request is stated in categorical terms and requires an extensive search for the records requested.

X  The requested records have not yet been located and additional time is needed to complete the search.

X  The requested records require examination and evaluation to determine if the documents are exempt from disclosure and/or must be redacted.

X  The requested records cannot be provided within the prescribed time frame without causing an undue burden or interfering with the operation of ISP.

Consultation is needed with another public body in order to respond to the request.

ISP handles requests for video records separately. Depending on the records you requested, you may receive additional correspondence for either video or paper and audio records.

Sincerely,

Sarah Wheeler
Illinois State Police
Freedom of Information Officer
801 South 7th Street, Suite 1000-S
Springfield, Illinois 62703
ISP.FOIA.Officer@Illinois.gov

title: Data on top employers of SNAP recipients

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Data on top employers of SNAP recipients
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

Data on the employers in the state of Minnesota with more than 100 workers receiving benefits from the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP), commonly referred to as food assistance.

Specifically, I request the following information, if available:
- The name of the employer
- The total number of the employer's workers who are currently receiving SNAP benefits
- The time period this data covers (e.g., the most recent quarter or year for which data is available)

I understand that specific identifying information for individual benefit recipients is protected by privacy laws, and my request is solely for aggregated, non-personally identifiable data regarding employer affiliation.

Contact info: David Moore, co-founder of Sludge, a nonprofit newsroom
(917) 753-3462
readsludge@gmail.com
http://readsludge.com

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

David Moore
